reviews.review_description: Love this game. Give us more tracks 🙌

One of the best racing games I’ve played. Thank you for bringing a Podracer game like this. The controls are very good!

I just think it would be great if the boost could last longer before overheating. An in-game option to disable overheating would also be awesome!

I hope the game will include multiplayer soon!

First racing game I have downloaded on the quest and it's great fun. Obviously it's taken inspiration from the pod racing arcade game, which I was also a fan of.

Controls work really well on the Quest and are super easy to understand, wouldn't have any concerns if I had a child try to understand as the tutorial covers the basics very well.

Keep up the good work, sure it will be a great success.

Never before, have I ever seen a control system THIS bad in VR. I feel like some sort of congratulations are in order here. 

The game itself looks nice enough for App Lab and I'm sure the finished product's going to look even nicer... but that's a completely moot point, because the controls are completely horrible. It's a shame, really. Could have been a nice little racer with usable controls. And no, I'm not going to spend weeks slowly practicing with the unwieldy controls to "get the hang of it". I'm just going to give it one star and not play it any more. 

I'm sure the control system looked good on paper and the dev can handle it very well himself, since he's coded it. But for a regular user, just forget about it. This does not work in practice.

developer_response.body: That's a bit harsh, but the tutorial certainly could be better! I'm curious which part exactly you found unusable, hard to improve without knowing that, feel free to hop on discord and elaborate :)

reviews.review_description:
This is the most fantastically triumphant piece of virtual media I have ever experienced, and you, the most dearest reader, NEED to download all 513mg of this so called “Dart Racers” 
- love, Taco
This was a really fun experience that really gives you a great sense of speed. Maximizing my speed with boost and throttle felt so good. Definitely recommend to anyone who is into racing games!

I am so surprised how this game translates the feeling of a hover pod racer into VR. I had to get used to the steering in the beginning but managed within 1 or 2 minutes. I love how the feeling of drifting feels in this game. There is huge potential in this game if there are more tracks added. Another idea is to have some power-ups to pick up that cool down the boost or some parts of the track you drive over that do the same. Looking forward to how this game evolves.

developer_response.body:
Thanks! There will be more content, tracks and otherwise! 
The first small test track has heat/cool zones that modify the boost, but can be tricky to notice in middle of the race!

reviews.review_description: This is very close to Star Wars podraceing game

One of the best VR racing games I've played. Controls felt good, I only crashed because of my lack of skill. The tracks were good, a little basic at first but got more interesting, and the AI racers were challenging but reasonable. When more tracks get added, I'll be sure to try them out! 
I like having the leaderboards, super fun to go for the fastest time and even better to get it, but I'd also love to have online multi-player. Either way, very good game and for such a reasonable price there's no reason not to try it!

developer_response.body: Thanks, that means a lot! There are some fun goodies coming.

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing! And i am really looking forward to more updates, possibly including maybe adding haptic suit compatibility? But anyways, the sound is kind of distorted and fuzzy when the engine reaches a certain speed. It occasionally sounds like someone put the engine right next to the microphone for a short amount of time. It also does that for when i am casting to the meta app. Not sure what that means, it just sounds fuzzy and glitched at some spots of the audio. I hope you guys can fix that. Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body: Thanks! There will be a bunch of sound improvements in next release, hopefully it will help! What kind of haptic suit? Ping us on Discord, perhaps something can be done :)

reviews.review_description: I've had my Q2 for about 3 years now and have yet to find a racing game that is as addictive as Dart Racer is. Once I got the steering down I could not stop until I had set (what I think is the best time) for every course! Hats off to the Dev on this one. Join the Discord and post your times and I look forward to beating them!

Good graphics, nice playability but the controls require some buzz or vibration to determine edges (its strange some movements like up down, push and pull send you far and others didn't be recognized in every try) I recommend a mix of sound and vibration to determine limits of the "controllers" to provide better experience, or maybe light (green= go red= backwards or stop) its complicated drive and turn in general and the heat release its weird (it suppose I must left 1 handler to release heat? both? or never get in to overheat?) but with a few corrections the experience could be more challenging than frustrating. A tutorial race 'll be the best way to "get right" the UX to success in general races.

developer_response.body: Perhaps I'm misunderstanding, but there definitively is a short vibration when you reach max throttle in either direction. Maybe it is too subtle or the intended movement wasn't explained clearly enough? Hop on Discord to clear it up! At any rate, tutorial certainly will be improved in the future.

reviews.review_description: This game is awesome but my hands get tired quickly
